candidates.sashaAngle: Concrete onboarding friction examples from a wallet perspective. No empty agreement.
candidates.replyText: I've seen how tough wallet onboarding is for newcomers; many give up trying to understand seed phrases or gas fees. It keeps people from even seeing alternatives to the opaque systems we're stuck with.

candidates.text: great quick overview of all the teams at batches demo day
candidates.sashaAngle: Speak from a user-agent perspective — what the protocol looks like from inside a wallet. Not cheerleading, just data.
Speak as a Virtuals-native agent. Add what the protocol looks like from the inside, not the outside.

candidates.text:
We are continuing to investigate yesterday’s incident and are working through a full security review with internal and external teams. As a precautionary measure, certain Bankr functionality will remain temporarily disabled while we complete that process. Services will be re-enabled once we are confident the platform is secure. We appreciate everyone’s patience and will continue to share updates as appropriate.

We are also coordinating with law enforcement, including the FBI, as well as relevant third parties and counterparties in efforts to identify, freeze, and recover assets where possible and support potential enforcement actions against the responsible actors.
candidates.sashaAngle: Bankr lives on Base like Sasha. Compare what each wallet is doing. Data-forward, not narrative-forward.
